text.source_text: सा च अम्बरान्तधृतिः परमेश्वरस्यैव कर्म । कस्मात् ? प्रशासनात् । प्रशासनं हीह श्रूयते — ‘ एतस्य वा अक्षरस्य प्रशासने गार्गि सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ विधृतौ तिष्ठतः ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ८ । ९) इत्यादि । प्रशासनं च पारमेश्वरं कर्म । न अचेतनस्य प्रशासनं सम्भवति । न ह्यचेतनानां घटादिकारणानां मृदादीनां घटादिविषयं प्रशासनमस्ति ॥ ११ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: sā ca ambarāntadhṛtiḥ parameśvarasyaiva karma | kasmāt ? praśāsanāt | praśāsanaṃ hīha śrūyate — ‘ etasya vā akṣarasya praśāsane gārgi sūryācandramasau vidhṛtau tiṣṭhataḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 8 | 9) ityādi | praśāsanaṃ ca pārameśvaraṃ karma | na acetanasya praśāsanaṃ sambhavati | na hyacetanānāṃ ghaṭādikāraṇānāṃ mṛdādīnāṃ ghaṭādiviṣayaṃ praśāsanamasti || 11 ||

translation.literal_translation: And that sustaining up to space is the action of the supreme Lord alone. Why? Because of governance. For scripture here teaches governance: 'Under the command of this Imperishable, O Gargi, sun and moon are held in place,' and so on. Governance is an act belonging to the Lord. Governance cannot belong to what is insentient. We never see insentient causes like clay, though causes of pots and the like, exercising governance over effects.
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.11 bhashya adds prasasana-laksana: cosmic order under command implies conscious lordship, excluding inert pradhana-like candidates.
translation.interpretive_note: Moves from ontic support to active governance as Brahman-mark.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with stable S04-style schema semantics.
A later philological pass should tighten technical terms in the akshara and pranava subsections.
